title: Garlicky Molten Comforting Venezuelan Dish Pilaf
description: A comforting Venezuelan dish featuring a garlicky and molten center, perfect for a low-carb friendly meal. This recipe uses spherification to create a fun and unique texture.
instructions: Heat the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the Arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, 1/2 cup at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each portion to absorb before adding the next.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 18-20 minutes or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted and well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Dust the top with icing sugar and use a kitchen torch to caramelize the sugar.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
To serve, use a spoon to create a hole in the center of the pilaf and serve with a side of salsa or guacamole.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
